requirement_text: Manufacturing license must be valid, traceable, and current for regulated operations
Each manufactured batch must have a complete Batch Manufacturing Record with batch identity, manufacturing date, and process steps
Finished products must have test results or Certificate of Analysis with parameters, results, and approval status
Raw materials must come from approved vendors and meet defined specifications
evidence_type: vendor approval record;material specification;purchase QA record

requirement_text: Critical equipment must have calibration records with last calibration and next due date
Deviations must be investigated and CAPA records must capture issue, root cause, corrective action, and closure status
Batch distribution must be traceable through dispatch and distributor records
Electronic records must maintain an audit trail with user, action, timestamp, and record change history
Each batch must be manufactured according to an approved Batch Manufacturing Record
Finished product quality must be verified before release through approved COA or QC documentation
Equipment used in production must be calibrated and maintained according to schedule
Raw materials must be procured from qualified vendors and verified against specifications
Deviation events must be documented with CAPA and tracked to closure
GMP records must preserve data integrity and audit trail history for critical operations
Scheduled formulations must comply with notified NPPA ceiling price
The marketed MRP must not exceed the applicable NPPA ceiling price after permitted adjustments
Price to retailer and downstream sale records must support pricing compliance review
Each priced product sale should be traceable by batch and manufacturer details
State manufacturing license must be valid and available for inspection
Inspection events must be documented with inspection date, inspector name, and observations
Inspection or review outcomes must capture the current compliance status of the facility or product
Product and batch records must identify the manufactured product, batch, and manufacturing date
Storage conditions relevant to licensed products must be documented and reviewable
Controlled documents must be versioned and traceable
User-linked timestamped records must exist for regulated documentation and changes
